title: mascagnia riedeliana
intro: Mascagnia riedeliana, also known as the Riedel's Mascagnia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. It is a climbing shrub native to South America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and providing relief from digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Mascagnia riedeliana include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally, depending on the specific health concern being addressed.
